results.title: Ben Stokes slams Lord's pitch despite England's win over New Zealand, says 'It doesn't help Test cricket...'
results.description: Despite England's 115-run win over New Zealand, Ben Stokes criticised the Lord's pitch, saying surfaces that produce such short matches do not benefit Test cricket. The MCC acknowledged concerns over the pitch's variable bounce, while Stokes stressed the importance of maintaining a fair contest between bat and ball in the longest format.

results.title: Inside Apple’s Siri Crisis: Delayed promises, leadership shakeup, and more
results.description: From ambitious AI plans to delayed rollouts, Apple's Siri upgrade has faced a turbulent road. Here's how the virtual assistant evolved through years of challenges, setbacks, and turning points.

results.title: The AI trade trap: Why successful tech stocks are triggering a trillion-dollar market meltdown in Korea, Taiwan
results.description: A sharp tech selloff exposed concentration risks in Asian markets, where chip giants such as TSMC, Samsung and SK Hynix dominate benchmarks. As their rapid gains pushed portfolio weights beyond risk limits, active fund managers were forced to sell despite strong fundamentals. The unwind triggered market volatility, accelerated fund outflows and intensified the shift from active to passive investing.

results.title: AICC Secretary Suraj Hegde Dies
results.description: All India Congress Committee (AICC) secretary Suraj Hegde passed away at 55 on Sunday at a private hospital following a cardiac arrest; top Congress leaders expressed deep grief over the sudden death, terming it as a big loss to both the state and the party

results.title: ‘Need more development’: Tourists seek better infrastructure at Manipur’s iconic Loktak Lake
results.description: Tourists are returning to Loktak Lake in Manipur as rising footfall signals a gradual return to normalcy after months of disruption since May 2023. Visitors are seen boating at Northeast India’s largest freshwater lake as local stakeholders highlight the need for better infrastructure to boost tourism and economic recovery.#LoktakLake #Manipur #NortheastIndia #IndiaTourism #TravelIndia #ManipurNews #TourismRecovery #Imphal #EcoTourism #IndiaNews #Phumdis #BreakingNews

results.title: This bronze medal is huge confidence booster: Coach Rani Rampal on India U18 women's Asia Cup campaign
results.description: New Delhi, June 8 (IANS) Indian U-18 women's hockey team coach Rani Rampal termed the team's Asia Cup bronze medal a "huge confidence booster," even as captain Sweety Kujur expressed slight disappointment over missing out on the gold. The Indian junior eves put up a spirited display at the continental tournament, as they finished at the top of Pool A after winning all three of their matches in dominant fashion. The team scored an incredible 30 goals while conceding only two throughout the pool stage. However, after an intense and well-fought 2-2 draw over four quarters, they suffered a 1-3 defeat in shootout against China in their semi-final clash. But they secured the bronze medal after registering a commanding 3-0 victory over Korea in the third-place playoff. Speaking on the team's performance, head coach Rani stated that this tournament is merely the launching pad for the young squad. "This is just the beginning for these players, and they have a long hockey journey ahead of them. I believe this medal and the Asia Cup tournament have been a huge confidence booster for them, and it will help them perform even better in the future," she told IANS. Sandeepa Kumari (2’), Captain Sweety Kujur (16’) and Nousheen Naz (33’) were on target as India capped off their campaign with an impressive podium finish. However, skipper Sweety Kujur did not mince words about the team's ultimate target, admitting that self-inflicted errors cost them the top spot on the podium. "We are happy, but at the same time a little disappointed because we could not fully achieve the goal we had set for ourselves. We believed our team was capable of winning the gold medal, but we fell short because of our own mistakes," Kujur admitted while speaking to IANS. The bronze medal marks a strong finish to India’s campaign at the Women’s U18 Asia Cup 2026, with the team showcasing its attacking quality, scoring 36 goals, defensive resilience and immense promise throughout the tournament. --IANS bc/

results.title: Surat Webinar Explores Middle East Opportunities Through IJEX, Dubai
results.description: GJEPC’s Surat Regional Office organised a webinar titled “Unlock Middle East Opportunities through IJEX” on 5 June 2026, drawing more than 70 participants from the gem and jewellery industry. The session was led by Ashish Sakhardande, Head – IJEX, GJEPC, who shared insights into the UAE and Middle East jewellery market, highlighting emerging business opportunities... results.title: Kangana Ranaut's Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata Set for Theatrical Release
results.description: Kangana Ranaut is set to return to the big screen with her film Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata, inspired by the courageous nurses of Cama Hospital. Scheduled for theatrical release on June 12, the film will later stream on ZEE5. In an interview, Kangana highlighted the essential roles of nurses and the bravery they displayed during the Mumbai 26/11 attacks. The film, produced by Manikarnika Films and others, aims to honor these frontline workers. With a strong cast and a compelling story, excitement is building among fans. Stay tuned for more updates on its OTT release!

results.description: Two persons were killed, while five other suffered injuries in separate road accidents here in the past 24 hours. In the first incident, a 55-year-old unidentified pedestrian died after an SUV hit him near the busy intersection at Rajouri Garden Chowk around 3.50 am on Sunday.
